name: Advance America
description_short: Advance America is one of the largest short-term lenders in the US, offering payday loans, installment loans, and auto title loans at 800+ stores across 27 states.
description_long:
Founded in 1997 and headquartered in Spartanburg, South Carolina, Advance America grew into the largest payday lending chain in the United States by store count. The company is a founding member of the Community Financial Services Association of America (CFSA) and operates as a state-licensed consumer lender in every market it serves, subject to oversight from the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB). In February 2012, Advance America was acquired by Grupo Elektra — a subsidiary of Mexican conglomerate Grupo Salinas, owned by billionaire Ricardo Salinas Pliego — for approximately $780 million. The company is currently led by CEO J. Patrick O'Shaughnessy.

Advance America's core product is the payday loan: short-term cash advances of $100 to $1,800 (amounts vary by state and income), repaid on the borrower's next payday. APRs on payday loans range dramatically by state — approximately 460% in California and as high as 688.28% in less-regulated markets. The company also offers installment loans (up to $5,000, with some exceeding 200% APR), auto title loans (up to $25,000 based on vehicle value), and lines of credit. All products are available in-store and, in eligible states, online. Applications take minutes online; in-store processing typically wraps up in about 15 minutes. Online applicants must verify their bank account via Plaid, and funding can arrive same-day if approved before 10:30 AM Eastern.

With 800+ physical locations across 27 states, Advance America's footprint is unmatched in the payday lending space. Same-day funding — immediate in-store, same-day online — sets it apart from slower lenders. The company's online portal at web.advanceamerica.net allows customers to apply, monitor loan status, make debit card payments, and access live chat support. The BBB has granted the company an A+ rating with accreditation since 2024, and the Whittier, CA branch carries a 5.0/5 score across 538 Google Maps reviews, suggesting consistent in-store service quality.

The honest reality is that Advance America's products are among the most expensive forms of consumer credit available. APRs in the hundreds of percent mean a two-week payday loan can cost the equivalent of $15–$30 per $100 borrowed — manageable for a genuine one-time emergency, but financially damaging when rolled over repeatedly. The company's BBB customer review score sits at just 1.7/5 from 154 reviews, and 302 complaints were filed over the past three years, pointing to real friction around loan terms, fees, and collections. Advance America is best used as a last resort for genuine short-term emergencies — not as a recurring cash flow solution. Borrowers in states with APR caps (like Ohio's 28% ceiling on installment loans) get meaningfully better terms.

pricing.guarantee_details: Not applicable. Advance America is a lender — loan fees and interest are governed by state-specific loan agreements, not subscription or refund policies.

pros: Largest payday lender in the US by store count — 800+ locations in 27 states
Same-day funding available: immediate in-store, or same-day online if approved before 10:30 AM ET
In-store application process takes approximately 15 minutes start to finish
BBB A+ rating with accreditation since 2024
Founding member of the CFSA, demonstrating long-standing industry compliance commitments
Online portal supports applications, loan status, payments, and live chat — no branch visit required
Loan amounts up to $25,000 (auto title loans) for borrowers with vehicle equity
cons: Extremely high APRs — payday loans reach up to 688.28% and California borrowers face ~460% APR
BBB customer review score is only 1.7/5 from 154 reviews, sharply contrasting the A+ accreditation grade
302 BBB complaints filed in the past three years, with reported issues around fees, loan terms, and collections
No confirmed standalone mobile app — online access is via mobile-responsive website only
Only available in 27 states — borrowers in other states have no access
best_for: Underbanked individuals who cannot qualify for a traditional bank loan or credit card
Borrowers facing a genuine short-term cash emergency before their next paycheck
Customers who prefer in-person service and live near one of 800+ physical branch locations
People in states with APR caps (e.g., Ohio) where installment loan terms are significantly more affordable
diagnosis: Advance America is best for underbanked consumers who need fast cash — $100 to $1,800 — within hours and have no access to lower-cost credit options. The main caveat is steep cost: APRs routinely exceed 300–600% depending on the state, making these loans financially damaging if not repaid quickly. Borrowers with any access to a credit union PAL, personal loan, or employer advance should exhaust those options first.
services: Debit Card Loan Repayment via Online Portal
Loan Status and Account Management Portal

cfpb_data.cfpb_company_name: Advance America, Cash Advance Centers, Inc.
